title: phyteuma charmelii subsp. scheuchzeri
intro: Phyteuma charmelii subsp. scheuchzeri, also known as Alpine Toothwort or Scheuchzer's Rampion, is a plant species in the bellflower family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help with wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Phyteuma charmelii subsp. scheuchzeri include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its potential to support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Phyteuma charmelii subsp. scheuchzeri include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for various health remedies.
